name: elemental-earth-node
summary: A pocket of the Elemental Plane of Earth. The room is not a room — it is a rough cavity in living stone where crystal grows faster than moss, and gravity seems to pick its own direction.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: irregular — the 'room' is wherever the stone parted

accent.purpose: crystal-vein inlay running through the floor

primary_palette.note: a beating gemstone heart of the node
a rough monolith grown, not carved

story_prompts: a dao surveys the node, dispatching gem-workers
the core is cracking — a great earthquake is imminent on a linked material world
xorn have infested and are eating the prize crystals
a miner has been fused into the wall and is still alive, slowly becoming stone
empty, silent except for the slow growth-chime of crystal forming in real time

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim (0.25-0.4) but crystals provide bright cold-blue/violet point sources

anti_patterns: no worked stone, no furniture, no wood — this is raw geology
no fire sources — light comes from crystal, not flame
walls should be jagged; use trims to break any straight line
avoid symmetry entirely — nothing here was built to a plan
